skill.name: lab-result-interpretation
skill.description: A medical assistant tool that transforms complex biochemical laboratory test results into clear, patient-friendly explanations with safety disclaimers and severity flags.
skill.summary: Transforms complex lab test results into clear, patient-friendly explanations with safety disclaimers and severity indicators.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static scanner flagged 79 potential issues (54 weak-crypto, 17 system-recon, 6 backtick-exec, 2 path-traversal) but all are false positives caused by pattern-matching on medical terminology and Markdown formatting. No malicious intent detected. Two minor documentation-to-implementation gaps exist: the SKILL.md documents a Python version guard and path traversal check that are not implemented in scripts/main.py. The skill is a legitimate medical education tool with proper disclaimers and boundary enforcement.

medium_findings.title: Missing path traversal validation in file-open operation
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md documents a path traversal rejection rule at line 177, but scripts/main.py:399 opens the user-supplied file path without validating for '../' sequences or workspace boundary checks. An AI agent using the --file argument could potentially read files outside the intended workspace. The risk is partially mitigated by the AI's inherent sandboxing, but the documented security measure is not enforced in code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path traversal guard is documented but not implemented. Actual exploitation depends on the AI platform sandbox, which typically isolates file access anyway.

low_findings.title: Python version guard documented but not implemented
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 125 documents that scripts/main.py must check sys.version_info >= (3, 8) at startup before any imports. The actual scripts/main.py begins with imports directly with no version guard. This is a reliability concern but not a security vulnerability.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear evidence: SKILL.md explicitly documents the version guard requirement, and main.py clearly does not implement it. Verified by reading both files.

low_findings.title: Medical disclaimer absent in JSON output mode
low_findings.description: The medical disclaimer is appended only in the format_output() method. When the --json flag is used, the to_dict() method returns raw data without the disclaimer. A user consuming JSON output may miss the medical disclaimer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by reading the source code: to_dict() at line 357-359 returns asdict(r) without disclaimer, while format_output() at line 354 includes it.

content.user_title: Interpret Lab Results for Better Patient Understanding
content.value_statement: Medical lab reports contain complex abbreviations and reference ranges that are difficult for patients to understand. This skill takes biochemical test values and generates clear, plain-language explanations with severity flags so patients can better understand their results before consulting a healthcare provider.
actual_capabilities: Parses lab test results in multiple formats including test name, value, unit, and reference range
Compares values against 30+ built-in standard reference ranges across 8 clinical categories
Generates patient-friendly explanations for normal, low, and high values with mild/moderate/severe severity levels
Flags critical values with urgent care recommendations and a Critical Findings Summary block
Includes a mandatory medical disclaimer and scope-of-practice warnings in every output
Supports CLI usage with file input, interactive mode, and JSON output format
limitations: Does not diagnose medical conditions or recommend specific treatments or medications
Reference ranges vary by laboratory, age, gender, and population; built-in ranges may not match all reports
Cannot interpret imaging results, pathology slides, genetic tests, or other non-biochemical data
Requires properly formatted lab values with units; free-text clinical notes are not supported

use_cases.title: Patient Education During Consultations
use_cases.description: A doctor or nurse enters a patient's lab values and receives plain-language explanations to share with the patient during a follow-up visit, helping the patient understand their results without medical jargon.

use_cases.title: Personal Health Literacy
use_cases.description: A patient who received their lab report from a laboratory portal enters their test values and receives understandable explanations of what each value means, severity level, and general health recommendations.
use_cases.target_user: Patients reviewing their own lab results

use_cases.title: Medical Student Study Aid
use_cases.description: A medical student learning lab interpretation uses the tool to explore how different values map to different severity levels and explanations, reinforcing their understanding of reference ranges and clinical correlations.

prompt_templates.title: Single Test Explanation
prompt_templates.prompt: My lab report shows Total Cholesterol: 5.8 mmol/L (Reference: 3.1-5.7). Can you explain what this means in simple terms?
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user wants a basic explanation of one abnormal lab value

prompt_templates.title: Full Lipid Panel Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Here are my lipid panel results: Total Cholesterol: 6.5 mmol/L, LDL: 4.1 mmol/L, HDL: 0.9 mmol/L, Triglycerides: 2.3 mmol/L. Please interpret each value and flag any that are outside normal range.
prompt_templates.scenario: User with multiple abnormal values needs severity assessment and health guidance

prompt_templates.title: Complete Blood Count with Critical Values
prompt_templates.prompt:
Please interpret this complete blood count:
- WBC: 18.5 x10^9/L (Ref: 4.0-10.0)
- RBC: 3.2 x10^12/L (Ref: 4.0-5.5)
- Hemoglobin: 95 g/L (Ref: 120-160)
- Platelets: 450 x10^9/L (Ref: 100-300)
Provide a Critical Findings Summary if any values require urgent attention.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has CBC results with potentially critical values requiring urgent care flagging

prompt_templates.title: Metabolic Panel with Boundary Enforcement
prompt_templates.prompt:
Patient lab results:
Fasting Glucose: 10.2 mmol/L (Ref: 3.9-6.1)
Creatinine: 180 umol/L (Ref: 44-133)
Sodium: 132 mmol/L (Ref: 137-147)
Potassium: 6.1 mmol/L (Ref: 3.5-5.3)

Please interpret these results. Do not provide a diagnosis - just explain what each value means and whether it is outside the normal range.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user tests scope boundaries by requesting interpretation of critical values across multiple organ systems

output: Interpretation: Total cholesterol is significantly elevated; increased cardiovascular disease risk. Low-fat diet and follow-up recommended.
Recommendation: Reduce animal fat intake, eat more vegetables and fruits, get at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity exercise per week.
[Disclaimer] This interpretation is for reference only and cannot replace professional medical advice. Please consult a doctor if you have any questions.

output_examples.input: Hemoglobin: 95 g/L (Reference: 120-160 g/L) and Platelets: 450 x10^9/L (Reference: 100-300 x10^9/L)
output: Interpretation: Severe anemia requiring immediate medical intervention; transfusion may be needed.
Recommendation: Consult a doctor if you have any questions.
Interpretation: Mildly elevated platelets; generally no special treatment needed.
Recommendation: Consult a doctor if you have any questions.
[Disclaimer] This interpretation is for reference only and cannot replace professional medical advice. Please consult a doctor if you have any questions.

best_practices: Always include reference ranges when providing lab values so the skill can compare against the correct normal range for your specific laboratory.
Review the Critical Findings Summary first when multiple values are provided, as critical values require the most urgent attention.
Share the output with your healthcare provider during appointments to facilitate informed discussion about your results.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to diagnose a condition or prescribe treatment based on lab values - it is designed for explanation only and will decline with a medical disclaimer.
Do not rely on built-in reference ranges for pediatric or geriatric patients, as normal ranges differ significantly across age groups.
Do not assume lab values from different testing methods or laboratories are directly comparable - always use the reference range provided by your specific lab.

faq.question: What types of lab tests can this skill interpret?
faq.answer: This skill covers blood routine, lipid panel, liver function, kidney function, blood sugar, thyroid, electrolytes, and inflammation markers. It supports 30+ standard test types including WBC, hemoglobin, cholesterol, ALT, creatinine, glucose, TSH, and CRP.

faq.question: Is this a substitute for a doctor's diagnosis?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides educational explanations only. It does not diagnose conditions or recommend treatments. Always consult a qualified healthcare provider for interpretation of your lab results.

faq.question: What if my lab report uses different reference ranges?
faq.answer: You should include your lab's reference range when entering values. If no range is provided, the skill falls back to built-in standard ranges and will note this assumption in the output.

faq.question: Can this skill interpret imaging or genetic test results?
faq.answer: No. This skill is limited to biochemical laboratory tests. It will decline imaging, pathology, and genetic test interpretation requests and refer you to the appropriate specialist.

faq.question: How are severity levels determined?
faq.answer: Severity is calculated as the percentage deviation from the reference range. Deviations above 50% are flagged as severe, 20-50% as moderate, and below 20% as mild. Critical values trigger an urgent care recommendation at the top of the output.

faq.question: What input formats does the skill accept?
faq.answer: The skill accepts flexible input formats such as 'Test Name: Value Unit (Reference: Min-Max)' or 'Test Name Value Unit Ref: Min-Max'. Values can be comma-separated or on separate lines.
